properties.remark: TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN AN OPEN FIELD EAST OF THE INTERSECTION OF STATE ROUTE 39 AND COUNTY ROAD 1150 SOUTH. THE TORNADO MOVED NORTHEAST CAUSING EXTENSIVE DAMAGE TO A GROVE OF TREES AND SNAPPED 4 POWER POLES BEFORE LIFTING NORTHEAST OF THE INTERSECTION OF COUNTY ROADS 100 WEST AND 1150 SOUTH. THE TORNADO WAS DETERMINED TO BE AN EF-0 WITH PEAK WIND 85 MPH. THE PATH LENGTH WAS 0.9 MILES AND THE WIDTH WAS 100 YARDS.

TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN AN OPEN FIELD SOUTHWEST OF THE INTERSECTION OF COUNTY ROAD 1600 SOUTH AND 900 WEST MOVING EAST WHERE IT CAUSED SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE TO A 120FT BY 80FT POLE BARN WITH 80 PERCENT OF THE ROOF REMOVED AND THROWN INTO AN ADJACENT FIELD. THE TORNADO WAS DETERMINED TO BE AN EF-0 WITH PEAK WIND 85 MPH. THE PATH LENGTH WAS 0.1 MILES AND THE WIDTH WAS 50 YARDS.

TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN AN OPEN FIELD EAST OF THE INTERSECTION OF STATE ROUTE 39 AND COUNTY ROAD 1150 SOUTH. THE TORNADO MOVED NORTHEAST CAUSING EXTENSIVE DAMAGE TO A GROVE OF TREES AND SNAPPED 4 POWER POLES BEFORE LIFTING NORTHEAST OF THE INTERSECTION OF COUNTY ROADS 100 WEST AND 1150 SOUTH. THE TORNADO WAS DETERMINED TO BE AN EF-0 WITH PEAK WIND 85 MPH. THE PATH LENGTH WAS 0.9 MILES AND THE WIDTH WAS 100 YARDS.

TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN AT THE NORTH END OF SUNDANCE ROAD JUST SOUTH OF US-12. THE TORNADO HEAVILY DAMAGED A BARN AND PARTIALLY REMOVED THE ROOF OF A CHURCH. TREES WERE ALSO UPROOTED. THE TORNADO LIFTED NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF M-62 AND US-12. TORNADO WAS RATED EF-0 WITH PEAK WINDS OF 75 MPH. THE PATH LENGTH WAS 1.4 MILES WITH A WIDTH OF 150 YARDS.
MULTIPLE TREES BLOWN DOWN/UPROOTED. SIGNS WERE BLOWN OVER. WOODEN FENCES BLOWN DOWN, ROOF DAMAGE.

EM REPORTED WIND DAMAGE IN THE REGION SOUTH OF US 12, NORTHWEST OF M 62, AND EAST OF SECTION STREET IN EDWARDSBURG. LARGE TREE, APPROXIMATELY 2-3 FT IN DIAMETER UPROOTED ON CHURCH STREET. BRANCHES AND LIMBS UP TO 1 FT DIAMETER DOWNED AT RAILROAD CROSSING IN TOWN. LARGE TREES ON MAIN STREET (1-2 FT IN DIAMETER) DAMAGED A DECK AT ONE HOUSE. ANOTHER HOUSE ON MAIN STREET HAD LARGE LIMBS THROUGH A WINDOW WITH A FEW 3-6 LIMBS STICKING OUT OF THE SIDING OF THE HOUSE.
